essence: Installs, tests, and maintains fire‑sprinkler systems to protect buildings and occupants from fire.

identity.title: Fire Sprinkler Technician
identity.summary: Installs, inspects, and repairs fire‑sprinkler systems, ensuring they operate correctly and comply with fire‑safety regulations.

salary.notes: Technicians specialising in large commercial systems or complex suppression networks can exceed £50k-£60k+.

qualifications: Training in fire‑sprinkler installation and maintenance
Knowledge of BS EN 12845 and fire‑safety regulations
Ability to test, fault‑find, and certify sprinkler systems
Understanding of pipework, pumps, valves, and control systems

lifestyle.description: Fire sprinkler technicians work across homes, commercial buildings, and industrial sites installing and maintaining sprinkler systems. The role involves pipework, testing, fault‑finding, and ensuring compliance with safety standards. It suits individuals who enjoy technical work, problem‑solving, and contributing to building fire protection.

habits: Follows strict installation and safety standards
Keeps accurate maintenance and inspection records
Communicates clearly with building managers and contractors
